tasks.title: Setup Monorepo Structure and Base Configuration
tasks.description: Create the foundational monorepo structure with proper package organization and build tooling
tasks.details: Set up a monorepo using tools like Lerna or Nx with packages for core, parser, transformer, generator, validator, config, and plugins. Configure TypeScript with strict settings, ESLint, Prettier, and Jest. Create shared types and interfaces. Set up package.json scripts for building, testing, and publishing. Implement proper module resolution and dependency management between packages.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify all packages build successfully, TypeScript compilation works across packages, shared types are properly imported, and basic CLI structure is functional

subtasks.title: Configure workspace configuration
subtasks.description: Set up the monorepo workspace configuration using npm workspaces, defining the package structure and workspace-level dependencies
subtasks.details:
Create package.json with workspaces configuration, set up .npmrc for workspace management, configure hoisting rules, and establish workspace-level scripts for common operations
<info added on 2025-06-24T19:01:38.808Z>
Workspace configuration is now complete. Implemented npm workspaces in root package.json with proper workspace definitions. Configured .npmrc with appropriate hoisting rules for dependency management. Established base TypeScript configuration with composite project setup to support monorepo builds. Created complete package directory structure with individual packages: core (main library), parser (component parsing), transformer (code transformation), generator (output generation), validator (validation logic), config (configuration management), plugins (extensibility), and types (shared TypeScript definitions). Each package includes properly configured package.json files with correct dependencies and build scripts. Comprehensive TypeScript type definitions added to types package including UIKitAdapter interface for framework integration, WillowConfig schema for configuration validation, AST node types for parsing, Plugin interfaces for extensibility, Validation result types, Component/Style mapping definitions, and Transform operation interfaces. All packages are properly linked and ready for development.
</info added on 2025-06-24T19:01:38.808Z>

subtasks.title: Create package structure
subtasks.description: Establish the monorepo package structure with proper directory organization for different package types
subtasks.details: Create packages/ directory structure, set up individual package.json files for each package, define package naming conventions, and establish clear package boundaries and responsibilities

subtasks.title: Set up TypeScript configuration
subtasks.description: Configure TypeScript for the monorepo with proper module resolution and package references
subtasks.details: Create root tsconfig.json with composite projects, set up individual tsconfig.json files per package with proper references, configure path mappings for inter-package imports, and establish build order

subtasks.title: Configure linting and formatting
subtasks.description: Set up ESLint and Prettier with monorepo-aware configurations for consistent code style
subtasks.details: Install and configure ESLint with TypeScript support, set up Prettier with consistent formatting rules, create shared ESLint configs, configure lint-staged and husky for pre-commit hooks

subtasks.title: Set up testing framework
subtasks.description: Configure Jest or Vitest for monorepo testing with proper module resolution and coverage
subtasks.details: Install testing framework with TypeScript support, configure test runners for each package, set up shared test utilities, establish coverage thresholds, and create test scripts for individual and all packages

subtasks.title: Configure build tooling
subtasks.description: Set up build tools for compiling and bundling packages with proper dependency handling
subtasks.details: Configure build tools (tsup/esbuild/rollup) for package compilation, set up watch mode for development, create build scripts with proper dependency ordering, configure package exports and module formats

tasks.title: Define Core Interfaces and Type System
tasks.description: Create comprehensive TypeScript interfaces for all core abstractions including UI kit adapters, configuration schema, and plugin system
tasks.details: Define UIKitAdapter interface with methods for component transformation, style mapping, and validation. Create WillowConfig schema with support for UI kit selection, design system configuration, feature flags, and plugin definitions. Implement ComponentMap, StyleMap, TokenMap interfaces. Create AST node types and transformation interfaces. Use Zod or similar for runtime schema validation.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for interface compliance, schema validation tests with valid/invalid configurations, type checking tests to ensure interfaces work correctly across packages

subtasks.title: UIKitAdapter interface design
subtasks.description: Design the core UIKitAdapter interface that will serve as the contract for all UI kit integrations
subtasks.details: Create a comprehensive interface that defines methods for component transformation, style mapping, token resolution, and runtime validation. Include async support for dynamic imports and configuration options.

subtasks.title: WillowConfig schema definition
subtasks.description: Define the WillowConfig schema that will configure the design system behavior and UI kit integration
subtasks.details: Design a flexible configuration schema supporting multiple UI kits, custom tokens, component overrides, theme definitions, and build-time optimizations. Include TypeScript interfaces and validation schemas.

subtasks.title: Component mapping interfaces
subtasks.description: Create interfaces for mapping Willow components to target UI kit components
subtasks.details: Define types for component property mapping, event handler translation, slot/children handling, and component composition patterns. Support both one-to-one and one-to-many mappings.

subtasks.title: Style/token mapping interfaces
subtasks.description: Design interfaces for mapping design tokens and styles between Willow and target UI kits
subtasks.details: Create types for color token mapping, spacing scales, typography systems, breakpoint definitions, and custom CSS property handling. Include support for runtime theme switching.

subtasks.title: AST node type definitions
subtasks.description: Define TypeScript types for AST nodes used in code transformation
subtasks.details: Create comprehensive type definitions for JSX elements, import statements, component props, style objects, and template literals. Support TypeScript and JavaScript AST formats.

subtasks.title: Transformation interfaces
subtasks.description: Design interfaces for the transformation pipeline that converts Willow components to target UI kits
subtasks.details: Define types for transformation rules, visitor patterns, code generation options, and transformation context. Include hooks for custom transformations and middleware support.

subtasks.title: Runtime validation setup
subtasks.description: Set up runtime validation types and interfaces for development-time checks
subtasks.details: Create types for validation rules, error reporting, prop validation, style validation, and development warnings. Include integration with TypeScript's type system for compile-time checks.

tasks.title: Implement Configuration System
tasks.description: Build a robust configuration system that supports loading, merging, and validating configurations from multiple sources
tasks.details: Create configuration loader that supports JSON, YAML, and JavaScript config files. Implement configuration merging with precedence (global < project < CLI flags). Add environment variable support with WILLOW_ prefix. Create configuration validation using schemas from task 2. Support configuration inheritance and overrides. Implement caching for performance.
tasks.testStrategy: Test configuration loading from different file types, verify merging precedence works correctly, validate schema enforcement, test environment variable integration, and performance benchmarks

subtasks.title: Implement file loader for multiple formats
subtasks.description: Create a file loader that can read and parse configuration files in JSON, YAML, and JS/TS formats
subtasks.details: Build a flexible file loader module that detects file format based on extension and uses appropriate parsers. Support .json, .yaml, .yml, .js, and .ts extensions. Handle file reading errors gracefully and provide meaningful error messages.

subtasks.title: Build configuration merging logic with precedence rules
subtasks.description: Implement deep merging functionality that combines configurations from multiple sources according to defined precedence
subtasks.details: Create a deep merge utility that properly handles nested objects and arrays. Implement precedence order: CLI args > environment variables > local config files > global config files > defaults. Ensure later sources override earlier ones correctly.

subtasks.title: Add environment variable support and mapping
subtasks.description: Implement environment variable parsing and mapping to configuration properties
subtasks.details: Create a system to read environment variables with a specific prefix (e.g., WILLOW_) and map them to configuration paths. Support nested property mapping using delimiters (e.g., WILLOW_API_KEY maps to config.api.key). Handle type conversion for boolean and numeric values.

subtasks.title: Integrate validation system with configuration loading
subtasks.description: Connect the configuration loading system with the existing validation framework to ensure loaded configs are valid
subtasks.details: After merging configurations from all sources, pass the final configuration through the validation system. Provide clear error messages indicating which configuration source contains invalid values. Support partial validation for development environments.

subtasks.title: Implement configuration caching mechanism
subtasks.description: Add caching layer to avoid repeated file reads and parsing for unchanged configuration files
subtasks.details: Create a caching system that stores parsed configurations in memory. Implement cache invalidation based on file modification times. Add option to disable caching for development. Include cache warming functionality for production deployments.

tasks.title: Build AST Parser Module
tasks.description: Implement AST parsing capabilities using TypeScript compiler API for reliable code analysis
tasks.details: Use TypeScript compiler API (ts.createProgram, ts.createSourceFile) to parse TypeScript and JavaScript files. Implement component detection by analyzing JSX elements and React component patterns. Build import graph construction to track dependencies. Create scope analysis for proper symbol resolution. Handle edge cases like dynamic imports, re-exports, and conditional imports. Support both TS and JS with proper type stripping.
tasks.testStrategy: Test parsing of complex TypeScript/JavaScript files, verify component detection accuracy, validate import graph construction, test scope analysis with nested scopes, and benchmark parsing performance

subtasks.title: TypeScript compiler API setup
subtasks.description: Initialize and configure the TypeScript compiler API for the validation tool
subtasks.details: Set up the TypeScript compiler API, configure compiler options, create a program instance, and establish the basic infrastructure for parsing TypeScript/TSX files. Include proper error handling and configuration management.

subtasks.title: Component detection logic
subtasks.description: Implement logic to detect React components in TypeScript/JavaScript files
subtasks.details: Create detection patterns for function components, class components, arrow function components, and exported components. Handle various naming conventions and component declaration patterns including default exports and named exports.

subtasks.title: JSX element parsing
subtasks.description: Parse JSX elements to extract component usage and properties
subtasks.details: Implement JSX parsing to identify component usage, extract props, children, and nested components. Handle self-closing tags, fragments, and JSX expressions. Build a comprehensive AST traversal system for JSX elements.

subtasks.title: Import graph construction
subtasks.description: Build a dependency graph from import statements across the codebase
subtasks.details: Parse import statements to construct a directed graph of component dependencies. Handle various import types including default imports, named imports, namespace imports, and aliased imports. Track import paths and resolve relative/absolute paths.

subtasks.title: Scope analysis implementation
subtasks.description: Analyze variable scopes and component accessibility within modules
subtasks.details: Implement scope analysis to determine where components are accessible, handle module boundaries, track variable declarations and their usage contexts. Consider lexical scoping, module exports, and closure contexts.

subtasks.title: Edge case handling
subtasks.description: Handle complex scenarios like dynamic imports, re-exports, and conditional exports
subtasks.details: Implement handling for dynamic imports, barrel exports, re-exports, conditional exports, lazy loading, and code splitting scenarios. Handle TypeScript specific features like type exports and namespace merging.

subtasks.title: Type stripping for JS
subtasks.description: Process JavaScript files by stripping TypeScript type annotations
subtasks.details: Implement type stripping functionality to handle JavaScript files that may contain JSX but no TypeScript types. Ensure compatibility with both .js/.jsx and .ts/.tsx files. Handle flow type annotations if present.

subtasks.title: Parser optimization
subtasks.description: Optimize parsing performance for large codebases
subtasks.details: Implement caching mechanisms, incremental parsing, parallel processing where possible, and memory optimization. Add progress reporting for long-running operations and implement early termination for validation passes.

tasks.title: Create AST-Based Transformer Module
tasks.description: Replace regex-based transformations with reliable AST transformations for code modifications
tasks.details: Implement AST transformers using TypeScript compiler API transformer functions. Create import path resolution and rewriting logic. Build component prop mapping transformations. Implement style transformation capabilities. Handle complex scenarios like default imports, namespace imports, and aliased imports. Create rollback mechanisms for failed transformations. Support both in-place and copy transformations. Style transformation engine is significantly advanced with 8 out of 10 subtasks complete, including CSS-in-JS parsers/transformers, Tailwind class transformer, CSS modules handler, theme token migration, property renaming engine, styled-components transformer, emotion transformer, and style validation/optimization system (90% complete).
tasks.testStrategy: Test import transformations with various import patterns, verify component prop mapping accuracy, validate style transformations, test rollback mechanisms, and compare performance against regex-based approach. Focus on completing style transformation test fixes and comprehensive end-to-end testing.

subtasks.title: Design transformer API interface
subtasks.description: Define the core API structure for transformation operations including method signatures, configuration options, and error handling patterns
subtasks.details: Create TypeScript interfaces for transformer classes, define configuration schema, establish error handling patterns, and design plugin architecture for extensibility

subtasks.title: Implement import transformation logic
subtasks.description: Build the core logic to parse and transform import statements using AST manipulation
subtasks.details: Use TypeScript compiler API or Babel to parse import statements, identify source modules, transform import paths, and handle various import syntaxes (default, named, namespace)

subtasks.title: Implement style transformation engine
subtasks.description: Build system to transform CSS-in-JS styles, class names, and style properties
subtasks.details:
Style transformation engine is significantly advanced with 8 out of 10 subtasks complete. All major components implemented: CSS-in-JS parsers/transformers, Tailwind class transformer, CSS modules handler, theme token migration, property renaming engine, styled-components transformer, emotion transformer, and style validation/optimization system (90% complete with robust architecture). Currently focused on completing test fixes for subtask 5.4.9 and then moving to 5.4.10 for comprehensive end-to-end style transformation tests.
<info added on 2025-06-25T08:18:25.853Z>
Implementation Status: Style validation and optimization system is 90% complete with robust architecture in place. Built BaseStyleValidator with pluggable validation rules covering CSS properties, values, colors, units, best practices, and accessibility checks. BaseStyleOptimizer implemented with comprehensive strategies: duplicate removal, rule merging, property sorting, shorthand conversion, color optimization, unit optimization, and aggressive optimizations. Factory functions created for custom validators and optimizers. System supports validation severity levels (ERROR, WARNING, INFO) and optimization levels (NONE, BASIC, STANDARD, AGGRESSIVE). Test suite contains 29 tests with 26 passing - only 3 minor failures remain for magic number detection, z-index warnings, and color optimization edge cases. Core architecture functional and ready for integration.

subtasks.title: Develop rollback mechanism
subtasks.description: Create safe rollback system to undo transformations if issues occur
subtasks.details: Implement backup creation, change tracking, validation system, and restoration process. Include dry-run mode for preview before applying changes

subtasks.title: Build in-place vs copy transformation modes
subtasks.description: Implement options for transforming files in-place or creating copies
subtasks.details: Add configuration for transformation mode, implement file copying logic, handle file permissions, and provide progress tracking for both modes

subtasks.title: Create transformer composition system
subtasks.description: Build system to chain and compose multiple transformers for complex migration scenarios
subtasks.details: Design transformer pipeline architecture, implement transformer ordering, handle inter-transformer dependencies, and provide composition configuration options

subtasks.details: Develop comprehensive end-to-end tests that validate the complete style transformation pipeline from input to output. Test integration between all components and validate performance under real-world scenarios.

subtasks.details: Build mapping configuration system, implement prop name transformations, handle prop value conversions, and manage deprecated prop warnings. subtasks.title: Build prop deprecation handler

subtasks.title: Create prop validation system

subtasks.details:

<info added on 2025-06-25T07:50:29.880Z>
The base adapter architecture is now in place with the translateStyles method and StyleConfig interface implemented. The transformer package provides extensive style transformation capabilities including parsers and transformers for Tailwind, CSS-in-JS, and styled-components. The key remaining work is to integrate the transformer's style transformation capabilities with the adapter's runtime style translation system. The token conversion system has been successfully implemented with type-safe helpers, providing a foundation for mapping design tokens between different UI kits. Next steps involve creating the integration layer between the static transformation capabilities and runtime style translation.
</info added on 2025-06-25T07:50:29.880Z>
<info added on 2025-06-27T20:16:13.179Z>
Create a comprehensive BootstrapAdapter that extends the BaseAdapter class to bridge Bootstrap components and utilities with the Willow design system. Implement component mappings for core Bootstrap elements including Button, Card, Modal, Alert, and Form controls, ensuring proper prop translation and event handling. Build a utility class translation system that converts Bootstrap's spacing (m-, p-), color (text-, bg-), display (d-), and flexbox utilities to equivalent Willow/Tailwind classes. Integrate Bootstrap's 12-column grid system by mapping container, row, and col-* classes to Willow's responsive grid utilities. Develop a CSS variable conversion system that transforms Bootstrap's custom properties (--bs-primary, --bs-secondary, etc.) into Willow design tokens. Handle Bootstrap's JavaScript-dependent components by creating adapter wrappers for modals, tooltips, popovers, and dropdowns that maintain functionality while using Willow's component API. Provide comprehensive TypeScript definitions for all Bootstrap component props and ensure type safety across the adapter interface. Include thorough unit tests for component mapping accuracy, integration tests for complex scenarios, and end-to-end tests validating the complete migration workflow. Document common migration patterns, gotchas, and best practices for teams transitioning from Bootstrap to Willow, including examples of before/after code comparisons and automated migration tooling recommendations.
</info added on 2025-06-27T20:16:13.179Z>

<info added on 2025-06-25T07:49:58.415Z>
Status Update: Base adapter architecture implemented with mapComponent method and ComponentMapping interface established. Foundation is complete but core mapping functionality still requires implementation.

Remaining Implementation Tasks:
1. Prop transformation rules engine - Need to build dynamic property mapping system that can handle type conversions, name transformations, and conditional prop passing
2. Event handler mapping system - Implement event name translation and handler wrapping to normalize different UI kit event patterns
3. Children/slot conversion logic - Create system to handle different child content patterns (React children vs Vue slots vs Angular content projection)
4. Ref forwarding strategies - Develop consistent ref handling across different component architectures and frameworks

The architectural foundation provides the structure, but the actual adaptation logic that makes components interoperable between UI kits is the next critical implementation phase.
</info added on 2025-06-25T07:49:58.415Z>

- Full integration test suite validating end-to-end adapter functionality

subtasks.title: Create Bootstrap adapter
subtasks.description: Build adapter for Bootstrap components with utility class handling and complete Bootstrap 5 support
subtasks.details:
<info added on 2025-06-27T20:18:14.557Z>
Implement comprehensive Bootstrap 5 adapter for Willow CLI migration system with full component coverage and utility mapping.

**Core Implementation Tasks:**

1. **BootstrapAdapter Class Foundation**
   - Extend BaseAdapter with Bootstrap-specific configuration
   - Initialize Bootstrap CSS framework detection
   - Set up component registry and mapping tables
   - Configure default Bootstrap theme variables

2. **Component Mapping System**
   - Button: Map btn, btn-primary/secondary/success/danger/warning/info/light/dark, btn-sm/lg, btn-outline-* variants
   - Card: Map card, card-header, card-body, card-footer, card-title, card-text structures
   - Modal: Map modal, modal-dialog, modal-content, modal-header/body/footer with backdrop and fade transitions
   - Alert: Map alert, alert-primary/secondary/success/danger/warning/info/light/dark with alert-dismissible functionality
   - Form Controls: Map form-control, form-select, form-check, form-switch with validation states (is-valid, is-invalid)

3. **Utility Class Translation Engine**
   - Spacing: m-0 to m-5, mt/mb/ms/me-*, p-0 to p-5, pt/pb/ps/pe-* → Willow spacing tokens
   - Colors: text-primary/secondary/success/danger/warning/info/light/dark/muted, bg-* variants → Willow color system
   - Display: d-none/block/inline/inline-block/flex/inline-flex/table/table-row/table-cell → Willow display utilities
   - Flexbox: justify-content-*/align-items-*/flex-direction-*/flex-wrap-* → Willow flex system
   - Position: position-static/relative/absolute/fixed/sticky with top/bottom/start/end-* → Willow position system

4. **Grid System Integration**
   - Container: .container/.container-fluid → Willow container components
   - Row: .row with gutter system → Willow row with spacing
   - Columns: .col/.col-{breakpoint}-{size} (1-12) → Willow responsive grid columns
   - Breakpoint mapping: xs/sm/md/lg/xl/xxl → Willow breakpoint system

5. **CSS Variable Conversion System**
   - Primary color family: --bs-primary, --bs-primary-rgb → Willow primary tokens
   - Secondary colors: --bs-secondary, --bs-success, --bs-info, --bs-warning, --bs-danger → Willow semantic colors
   - Neutral colors: --bs-light, --bs-dark, --bs-body-bg, --bs-body-color → Willow surface and text tokens
   - Spacing: --bs-gutter-x, --bs-gutter-y → Willow spacing scale
   - Typography: --bs-font-family-base, --bs-font-size-base → Willow typography tokens

6. **JavaScript Component Lifecycle Management**
   - Modal: Handle show/hide events, backdrop clicks, keyboard navigation, focus management
   - Tooltip/Popover: Manage positioning engine, show/hide delays, trigger events (hover, focus, click)
   - Dropdown: Handle toggle states, keyboard navigation, outside click detection
   - Collapse: Manage accordion behavior, transition animations, multiple target support
   - Ensure proper cleanup and event listener removal

7. **TypeScript Integration**
   - Define BootstrapComponentProps interfaces for all mapped components
   - Create utility type helpers for Bootstrap variant unions
   - Implement strict typing for component mapping functions
   - Add generic types for theme customization and extension
   - Ensure full IntelliSense support for Bootstrap → Willow migrations

8. **Testing Strategy**
   - Unit tests: Each component mapping function with various prop combinations
   - Integration tests: Complex nested component structures (Card with Modal, Forms with validation)
   - Utility tests: All utility class translations with edge cases
   - JavaScript component tests: Lifecycle management and event handling
   - Snapshot tests: Generated Willow code output consistency

9. **Migration Documentation**
   - Component conversion examples: Bootstrap Button → Willow Button with all variants
   - Utility class cheat sheet: Complete mapping reference
   - Layout migration guide: Bootstrap grid → Willow grid system
   - JavaScript component migration: Event handling and lifecycle differences
   - Common patterns: Navigation bars, hero sections, card layouts, forms
   - Troubleshooting guide: Common migration issues and solutions

**Implementation Priority:**
Start with core components (Button, Card, Alert) and utility classes, then expand to complex JavaScript components and comprehensive testing coverage.
</info added on 2025-06-27T20:18:14.557Z>

subtasks.details: Write unit tests for core modules, test edge cases and error conditions, implement test coverage for utility functions, create tests for validation logic
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify unit test coverage meets >90% target and tests are comprehensive

subtasks.title: Create end-to-end integration scenarios
subtasks.description: TEST PHASE: Build comprehensive integration test scenarios covering main workflows
subtasks.details: Create CLI command integration tests, test file system operations, validate complete user workflows, test error handling and edge cases
subtasks.testStrategy: Ensure integration tests cover all critical user paths

subtasks.title: Execute performance benchmarks
subtasks.description: TEST PHASE: Run performance benchmarks and conduct analysis
subtasks.details: Execute benchmark test suites, analyze performance metrics, compare against baselines, identify performance bottlenecks, document performance characteristics
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate benchmark results show expected performance improvements

subtasks.title: Document testing best practices
subtasks.description: COMMIT PHASE: Create comprehensive testing documentation and patterns
subtasks.details: Document testing best practices, create testing pattern examples, write troubleshooting guides, establish testing workflow documentation
subtasks.testStrategy: Review documentation completeness and clarity

subtasks.title: Create test writing guide
subtasks.description: COMMIT PHASE: Develop test writing guide with examples and templates
subtasks.details: Create test writing templates, provide code examples for different test types, document testing conventions, create quick reference guides
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate guide usefulness with sample test implementations

subtasks.title: Final review and CI/CD pipeline setup
subtasks.description: COMMIT PHASE: Complete testing suite with CI/CD integration and final review
subtasks.details: Set up GitHub Actions workflows, configure automated test execution, integrate coverage reporting, set up performance regression monitoring, conduct final testing suite review
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify CI/CD pipeline works correctly and all tests pass consistently

tasks.title: Set up comprehensive pre-commit hooks with husky, lint-staged, vitest, ts-prune, and typedoc
tasks.description: Configure automated pre-commit hooks to enforce code quality, run tests, detect unused code, and generate documentation before each commit following Spec/Create/Test/Commit workflow
tasks.details: Implement comprehensive pre-commit hooks using a structured approach: SPEC PHASE - Define requirements and design execution flow with <10s performance target. CREATE PHASE - Install and configure husky, lint-staged, ESLint, Prettier, vitest, ts-prune, and typedoc with proper file pattern mappings. TEST PHASE - Validate hooks with intentional violations, benchmark performance, and verify CI compatibility. COMMIT PHASE - Document workflow, create troubleshooting guides, and establish bypass mechanisms. The implementation will use nano-tasks to ensure systematic completion of each phase while maintaining developer workflow efficiency.
tasks.testStrategy: Follow structured testing approach: Phase 1 - Test individual tool configurations in isolation. Phase 2 - Test integrated hook execution with various violation scenarios (linting errors, test failures, unused exports, documentation issues). Phase 3 - Benchmark execution time across different commit sizes (small: 1-3 files, medium: 4-10 files, large: 10+ files). Phase 4 - Verify bypass mechanism functionality and CI environment compatibility. Target: All tests pass with <10s execution time for typical commits.

subtasks.title: SPEC PHASE: Define pre-commit hook requirements and tool selection
subtasks.description: Establish comprehensive requirements for pre-commit hooks and finalize tool selection
subtasks.details: Document specific requirements for each tool: husky for Git hooks management, lint-staged for targeted file processing, ESLint/Prettier for code quality, vitest for testing, ts-prune for dead code detection, and typedoc for documentation. Define performance targets (<10s for average commits), file pattern matching strategies, and tool integration points. Specify bypass mechanisms and error handling requirements.
subtasks.testStrategy: Review requirements document with development team to ensure all use cases are covered and performance targets are realistic.

subtasks.title: SPEC PHASE: Design hook execution flow with performance targets
subtasks.description: Design the execution flow and optimization strategy for pre-commit hooks
subtasks.details: Create detailed execution flow diagram showing tool dependencies and parallel execution opportunities. Design performance optimization strategies including file pattern matching, incremental processing, and parallel tool execution. Specify error handling and recovery mechanisms. Document bypass procedures for emergency commits using --no-verify flag.
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate execution flow design through dry-run scenarios and performance modeling.

subtasks.title: SPEC PHASE: Specify tool configuration patterns for lint-staged
subtasks.description: Define specific configuration patterns and file matching rules for lint-staged
subtasks.details: Specify file pattern matching rules for different file types (.ts, .tsx, .js, .jsx, .json, .md). Define command sequences for each file type including ESLint, Prettier, vitest, ts-prune, and typedoc integration. Document configuration structure for .lintstagedrc.json including glob patterns and command arrays.
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate configuration patterns against project file structure and verify all relevant file types are covered.

subtasks.title: CREATE PHASE: Install and configure husky for Git hooks
subtasks.description: Install husky and establish Git hooks infrastructure
subtasks.details: Install husky@latest as dev dependency. Execute husky install to initialize Git hooks. Configure package.json with prepare script for automatic setup. Create .husky directory structure with proper permissions. Set up basic pre-commit hook template. Verify Git hooks integration and test basic functionality.
subtasks.testStrategy: Create test commit to verify husky intercepts Git operations and .husky directory structure is properly established.

subtasks.title: CREATE PHASE: Set up lint-staged with file pattern mappings
subtasks.description: Install and configure lint-staged with comprehensive file pattern matching
subtasks.details: Install lint-staged as dev dependency. Create .lintstagedrc.json with file pattern matching rules for different file types. Configure command sequences for TypeScript files, JavaScript files, JSON files, and Markdown files. Set up proper tool integration points for ESLint, Prettier, vitest, ts-prune, and typedoc.
subtasks.testStrategy: Stage various file types and run lint-staged manually to verify correct tool execution and file pattern matching.

subtasks.title: CREATE PHASE: Integrate ESLint, Prettier, vitest, ts-prune, and typedoc
subtasks.description: Install and configure all quality tools with proper integration
subtasks.details: Install ESLint, Prettier, vitest, ts-prune, and typedoc with required plugins and configurations. Create .eslintrc.json with TypeScript rules. Set up .prettierrc with project formatting standards. Configure vitest for --related flag usage. Set up ts-prune for unused export detection. Configure typedoc.json for documentation generation. Integrate all tools into lint-staged configuration.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test each tool individually and then in combination to ensure proper integration and no conflicts between tools.

subtasks.title: TEST PHASE: Test pre-commit hooks with intentional violations
subtasks.description: Validate hook functionality by testing with deliberate code quality violations
subtasks.details: Create test scenarios with intentional linting errors, formatting issues, test failures, unused exports, and documentation problems. Verify each violation is properly caught and blocked. Test that valid commits pass through successfully. Validate error messages are clear and actionable.
subtasks.testStrategy: Execute comprehensive test suite covering all violation types and verify appropriate hook behavior for each scenario.

subtasks.title: TEST PHASE: Benchmark hook execution time on various commit sizes
subtasks.description: Measure and optimize pre-commit hook performance across different scenarios
subtasks.details: Create benchmark scenarios with small (1-3 files), medium (4-10 files), and large (10+ files) commits. Measure execution time for each tool and total hook execution. Identify performance bottlenecks and implement optimizations. Verify <10s target is met for typical commits. Document performance characteristics.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run benchmark suite multiple times to establish baseline performance metrics and verify optimization effectiveness.

subtasks.title: TEST PHASE: Verify CI environment compatibility
subtasks.description: Ensure pre-commit hooks work correctly in CI/CD environments
subtasks.details: Test hook execution in CI environments including proper environment variable handling, dependency availability, and performance characteristics. Verify hooks can be bypassed when appropriate in CI. Test integration with existing CI workflows. Document any CI-specific configuration requirements.
subtasks.testStrategy: Execute full test suite in CI environment and verify all hooks function correctly with proper error handling and reporting.

subtasks.title: COMMIT PHASE: Document pre-commit workflow in contributing guide
subtasks.description: Create comprehensive documentation for the pre-commit workflow
subtasks.details: Update CONTRIBUTING.md with detailed pre-commit workflow documentation. Include tool descriptions, common workflows, troubleshooting steps, and bypass procedures. Document npm scripts for manual tool execution. Provide examples of typical commit scenarios and expected behavior.
subtasks.testStrategy: Review documentation with development team and verify all common use cases and workflows are clearly documented.

subtasks.title: COMMIT PHASE: Create troubleshooting guide for common issues
subtasks.description: Build comprehensive troubleshooting guide for pre-commit hook issues
subtasks.details: Document common pre-commit hook failures and their solutions. Include step-by-step resolution procedures for linting errors, test failures, configuration issues, and performance problems. Create FAQ section with frequent developer questions. Provide escalation procedures for complex issues.
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate troubleshooting guide by attempting to resolve common issues using only the documented procedures.

subtasks.title: COMMIT PHASE: Final review with bypass mechanism documentation
subtasks.description: Complete final review and document bypass mechanisms for emergency commits
subtasks.details: Conduct comprehensive review of entire pre-commit hook system. Document --no-verify bypass mechanism with clear guidelines on when and how to use safely. Create emergency commit procedures. Finalize all configuration files and documentation. Prepare deployment checklist for team rollout.
subtasks.testStrategy: Execute complete system review including all tools, documentation, and bypass mechanisms to ensure production readiness.
